tech.title: First U.S. satellite launch attempt fails
tech.description: At Cape Canaveral, the Vanguard TV3 rocket rises only a few feet before exploding, marking a highly publicized setback in the early American space program.

tech.title: Napster hit with landmark music-industry lawsuit
tech.description: Recording companies, led by the Recording Industry Association of America, file the A&M Records, Inc. v. Napster, Inc. lawsuit, accusing the file-sharing service of enabling massive copyright infringement and reshaping the future of digital music.

tech.title: Mars images hint at recent liquid water
tech.description: NASA releases Mars Global Surveyor photos that show changes in gullies, suggesting that liquid water may have flowed on the Martian surface in recent years.

history.title: 13th Amendment ratified, abolishing slavery in the U.S.
history.description: Georgia becomes the 27th state to approve the 13th Amendment, officially banning slavery and involuntary servitude throughout the United States.

history.title: Washington Monument construction completed
history.description: Engineers place a 3,300-pound capstone and an aluminum pyramidion atop the Washington Monument in Washington, D.C., finishing a 36-year building project and creating what was then the world’s tallest structure.

history.title: Monongah mine disaster kills hundreds in West Virginia
history.description: Explosions rip through coal mines in Monongah, West Virginia, killing hundreds of miners in what remains the deadliest mining disaster in U.S. history.

history.title: Halifax Explosion devastates Canadian port city
history.description: An ammunition-laden French ship collides with another vessel in Halifax Harbour and explodes, killing nearly 2,000 people and leveling much of the surrounding area.

history.title: Finland declares independence from Russia
history.description: Following the turmoil of the Russian Revolution, Finland’s parliament formally proclaims the country’s independence from the Russian Empire.

history.title: Irish Free State officially established
history.description: The Irish Free State comes into being as a self-governing dominion within the British Empire, implementing the terms of the Anglo-Irish Treaty and partitioning the island.

history.title: James Joyce’s novel Ulysses ruled not obscene
history.description: A U.S. federal judge in New York lifts the long-standing ban on James Joyce’s modernist novel Ulysses, clearing the way for its legal publication in the United States and expanding protections for literary expression.

history.title: ‘Blood in the Water’ Olympic water polo match
history.description: At the Melbourne Olympics, Hungary’s water polo team defeats the Soviet Union in a brutal, politically charged match that becomes a symbol of resistance after the crushed Hungarian Revolution.

history.title: Gerald Ford sworn in as U.S. Vice President
history.description: After the House and Senate approve his nomination under the Twenty-fifth Amendment, Gerald R. Ford takes the oath of office as vice president, replacing Spiro Agnew amid the unfolding Watergate scandal.

history.title: Saquinavir, first HIV protease inhibitor, approved
history.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approves Saquinavir, the first HIV protease inhibitor, helping usher in combination antiretroviral therapy that quickly drives down AIDS-related deaths.

history.title: U.S. recognizes Jerusalem as capital of Israel
history.description: The Trump administration formally announces that the United States recognizes Jerusalem as the capital of Israel, breaking with decades of U.S. policy and drawing strong international reaction.

crime_and_justice.title: Altamont Free Concert turns deadly
crime_and_justice.description: During a free Rolling Stones concert at Altamont Speedway in California, a young fan, Meredith Hunter, is stabbed to death by Hells Angels security, shocking the music world and symbolizing the dark turn of the 1960s.

crime_and_justice.title: École Polytechnique massacre in Montreal
crime_and_justice.description: An anti-feminist gunman opens fire at École Polytechnique in Montreal, murdering 14 women and wounding many others in one of Canada’s deadliest mass shootings.

crime_and_justice.title: Bombing of Colombia’s DAS building in Bogotá
crime_and_justice.description: A powerful car bomb detonates outside the Administrative Department of Security (DAS) headquarters in Bogotá, Colombia, killing dozens and injuring many more in an attack widely attributed to the Medellín cartel.

war_and_conflict.title: Central Powers capture Bucharest in World War I
war_and_conflict.description: During World War I, forces of the Central Powers capture Bucharest, the capital of Romania, dealing a major blow to the Allied position in Eastern Europe.

war_and_conflict.title: Battle of Taipale in the Winter War
war_and_conflict.description: On the Karelian Isthmus, Finnish defenders halt a Red Army advance at the Mannerheim Line during the Battle of Taipale, an early and symbolic engagement of the Winter War.

war_and_conflict.title: Heavy bombardment of Dubrovnik in the Yugoslav Wars
war_and_conflict.description: During the siege of Dubrovnik, Yugoslav People’s Army forces subject the historic Croatian city to one of the heaviest bombardments of the conflict, drawing international condemnation.

war_and_conflict.title: Babri Masjid demolished in Ayodhya
war_and_conflict.description: A large mob in Ayodhya, India, demolishes the 16th-century Babri Masjid mosque, sparking riots and communal violence across the country that leave more than a thousand people dead.

births.title: Craig Newmark born
births.description: American web entrepreneur Craig Newmark, founder of the online classifieds site Craigslist, is born in Morristown, New Jersey.

births.title: Andrew Cuomo born
births.description: Andrew Cuomo, future governor of New York and former U.S. secretary of Housing and Urban Development, is born in Queens, New York City.

births.title: Judd Apatow born
births.description: Future writer, director, and producer Judd Apatow, known for comedies like The 40-Year-Old Virgin and Knocked Up, is born in Syosset, New York.

births.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo born
births.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo, the Greek-Nigerian basketball star nicknamed the "Greek Freak" and future NBA champion and MVP, is born in Athens, Greece.

deaths.title: Jefferson Davis dies in New Orleans
deaths.description: Jefferson Davis, former U.S. senator and president of the Confederate States during the American Civil War, dies at age 81 in New Orleans, Louisiana.

deaths.title: Rock singer Roy Orbison dies
deaths.description: American singer-songwriter Roy Orbison, known for hits such as "Only the Lonely" and "Oh, Pretty Woman," dies of a heart attack at age 52.
